title: $2.5B Amazon Prime FTC Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who signed up for Amazon Prime between June 23, 2019 and June 23, 2025 through a challenged enrollment flow, or who attempted to cancel but were unable to do so.
eligibilityBullets: Signed up for Amazon Prime between June 2019 and June 2025
Enrolled through a challenged enrollment flow (e.g., shipping page, single-page checkout)
Used fewer than 10 Prime benefits in any 12-month period, or attempted to cancel unsuccessfully
summary: Amazon agreed to pay $2.5 billion to settle FTC allegations that it used dark patterns to trick millions of consumers into enrolling in Prime subscriptions and made cancellation unnecessarily difficult.
caseInfo: FTC v. Amazon.com Inc., Case No. 2:23-cv-00932-JHC

title: $7.25B Roundup Weed Killer Cancer Settlement
eligibility: U.S. residents exposed to Roundup weed killers before February 17, 2026, who were diagnosed with non-Hodgkin lymphoma.
eligibilityBullets: Used Roundup weed killer (occupational or residential)
Diagnosed with non-Hodgkin lymphoma
Exposed before February 17, 2026
proofDetails: Medical records showing non-Hodgkin lymphoma diagnosis and evidence of Roundup exposure history.
summary: Bayer proposed a $7.25 billion class settlement to resolve thousands of Roundup cancer lawsuits. Payouts range from $10,000 to $165,000+ depending on exposure type, age at diagnosis, and NHL severity.
caseInfo: 22nd Judicial Circuit Court, City of St. Louis, Missouri

title: $60.5M Tinder Age Discrimination Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who purchased Tinder Plus or Tinder Gold in California when over age 29 (on or after March 2, 2015) or over age 28 (on or after March 2, 2016) and were charged a higher price due to their age.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Tinder Plus or Tinder Gold in California
Were over age 29 (from March 2015) or over age 28 (from March 2016)
Were charged a higher subscription price due to age
proofDetails: If you did not receive a class notice, provide your name, contact info, approximate purchase date, state, date of birth or age, and estimated total amount paid with supporting documentation.
summary: Tinder agreed to pay $60.5 million to resolve claims it charged older users more for Tinder Plus and Gold subscriptions in California, violating the Unruh Civil Rights Act.
caseInfo: Candelore v. Tinder, Inc., Case No. BC583162, Superior Court of California, Los Angeles

title: $56.85M Wells Fargo CARES Act Settlement
eligibility: California mortgage holders whose accounts were current, who received a CARES Act forbearance on or after March 27, 2020, and whose accounts were reported as 'in forbearance' by Wells Fargo to a consumer reporting agency.
eligibilityBullets: Owned property in California with a Wells Fargo mortgage
Received CARES Act forbearance on or after March 27, 2020
Account was current but reported as 'in forbearance' to credit bureaus
summary: Wells Fargo agreed to pay $56.85 million to settle claims it violated the CARES Act by misreporting California mortgage accounts in COVID-19 forbearance, potentially harming borrowers' credit scores. Payments are automatic.
caseInfo: Stoff v. Wells Fargo Bank N.A., Case No. 37-2020-00020808-CU-BT-CTL

title: $90M Joint Juice False Advertising Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who purchased Joint Juice glucosamine supplements in California, Connecticut, Florida, Illinois, Maryland, Massachusetts, Michigan, Pennsylvania, or New York.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased Joint Juice glucosamine supplements
Resided in CA, CT, FL, IL, MD, MA, MI, PA, or NY at time of purchase
Purchase was during the applicable class period for your state
summary: Joint Juice agreed to pay $90 million total to settle claims it falsely advertised its glucosamine supplements' health benefits. Combined multi-state ($70M) and New York ($19.16M) settlements.
scope: Multi-state (CA, CT, FL, IL, MD, MA, MI, PA, NY)

title: $9.95M Norton LifeLock Robocall Settlement
eligibility: U.S. residents who received a prerecorded or artificial voice call about a LifeLock or Norton account from Gen Digital between February 19, 2021 and October 30, 2025, and did not have a LifeLock or Norton account at the time.
eligibilityBullets: Received a prerecorded/robocall about a Norton or LifeLock account
Call occurred between February 2021 and October 2025
Were NOT a Norton or LifeLock customer at the time of the call
summary: Gen Digital agreed to pay $9.95 million after placing approximately 300,000 prerecorded robocalls to wrong-number recipients about Norton or LifeLock accounts they never had. Each claimant gets $200-$625.
caseInfo: Jackson v. Gen Digital Inc., U.S. District Court, District of Arizona

title: $100M Walmart Spark Driver Pay Settlement
eligibility: Walmart Spark delivery drivers who lost earnings due to deceptive pay representations or withheld tips.
eligibilityBullets: Worked as a Walmart Spark delivery driver
Experienced deceptive earnings claims or withheld tips
Drove for Spark during the relevant time period
summary: Walmart agreed to pay $100 million to settle FTC and state charges that it misled Spark delivery drivers about earnings and withheld tips from gig workers.

title: $62.1M Hyundai & Kia Airbag Settlement
eligibility: Owners or lessees of certain Hyundai and Kia vehicles equipped with defective ZF-TRW airbag control units susceptible to electrical overstress.
eligibilityBullets: Owned or leased an eligible Hyundai or Kia vehicle
Vehicle equipped with a defective ZF-TRW airbag control unit
Use the VIN lookup tool on the settlement website to check eligibility
proofDetails: Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) of eligible vehicle and proof of ownership or lease.
summary: Hyundai and Kia agreed to a $62.1 million settlement over defective ZF-TRW airbag control units that could fail to deploy airbags in a crash due to electrical overstress.
caseInfo: In re: ZF-TRW Airbag Control Units Products Liability Litigation

title: $28M SiriusXM TCPA Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who received more than one telemarketing call from SiriusXM within any 12-month period between April 27, 2019 and October 31, 2025, despite registering with the Do Not Call Registry or requesting placement on SiriusXM's internal DNC list.
eligibilityBullets: Received 2+ telemarketing calls from SiriusXM within 12 months
Calls occurred between April 27, 2019 and October 31, 2025
Phone was on the Do Not Call Registry or SiriusXM's internal DNC list
summary: SiriusXM agreed to pay $28 million to settle TCPA claims for making unsolicited telemarketing calls to consumers on the Do Not Call Registry.
caseInfo: Campbell, et al. v. SiriusXM Radio Inc., Case No. 2:22-cv-2261-CSB-EIL

title: $14M Copa America Final Stadium Settlement
eligibility: Valid ticketholders to the Copa America Final on July 14, 2024 at Hard Rock Stadium who were denied entry or admitted but denied full access to their purchased seats.
eligibilityBullets: Held a valid ticket to the Copa America Final on July 14, 2024
Were denied entry to Hard Rock Stadium, OR
Were admitted but denied full access to facilities or purchased seats
proofDetails: Valid ticket and notice ID/PIN from settlement notification. Denied entry claims may require photo/video evidence.
summary: A $14 million settlement resolves claims that ticketholders to the 2024 Copa America Final were wrongfully denied entry or full access at Hard Rock Stadium in Miami. Up to $2,000 for denied entry, up to $100 for limited access.
caseInfo: Copa America Final Class Action, Miami

title: $5M Sprouts Farmers Market Receipt Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who used a credit or debit card at any Sprouts store between August 16, 2020 and October 31, 2022, or an EBT card between March 15, 2021 and April 15, 2023, and received a receipt showing more than the last 5 digits of their card number.
eligibilityBullets: Used a credit/debit card at Sprouts (Aug 2020 - Oct 2022) or EBT card (Mar 2021 - Apr 2023)
Received a receipt showing more than the last 5 digits of your card number
U.S. resident
proofDetails: If you received a notice with a claim ID, use the short-form claim. Otherwise, submit a long-form claim with a receipt or card statement.
summary: Sprouts agreed to pay $5 million to settle claims it printed more than the last five digits of customer card numbers on receipts, violating the Fair and Accurate Credit Transactions Act (FACTA).
caseInfo: Tran, et al. v. Sprouts Farmers Market Inc., Case No. 22STCV26572

title: $11.5M Cosequin Dog Supplement Settlement
eligibility: California residents who purchased Cosequin canine joint supplements for personal use between May 3, 2016 and May 6, 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Reside (or resided) in California
Purchased Cosequin dog joint supplements for personal use
Purchase was between May 3, 2016 and May 6, 2022
summary: Nutramax agreed to pay $11.5 million over claims it falsely marketed Cosequin dog supplements. No proof of purchase required. Up to $25 per unit purchased, max $150 per household.
caseInfo: Cosequin Dog Supplement Class Action, California

title: $200.2M Beef & Pork Worker Wage-Fixing Settlement
eligibility: Employees who worked at a beef or pork processing plant for JBS, Tyson, Cargill, Hormel, National Beef, Seaboard, Smithfield, or Perdue between January 1, 2000 and February 27, 2024.
eligibilityBullets: Worked at a beef or pork processing plant in the continental U.S.
Employed by JBS, Tyson, Cargill, Hormel, National Beef, or other defendants
Employment was between January 2000 and February 2024
summary: Nine settlements totaling $200.2 million resolve claims that major meat processors conspired to suppress worker wages through secret compensation meetings and data sharing.
caseInfo: Brown et al. v. JBS USA Food Company et al., Case No. 1:22-cv-02946

title: $17.5M American Express Antitrust Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who used a nonrewards Visa, Mastercard, or Discover credit card at qualifying retailers in Illinois between 2015 and 2022.
eligibilityBullets: Used a non-rewards Visa, Mastercard, or Discover credit card
Made purchases at qualifying Illinois retailers (Walmart, Kroger, Target, etc.)
Purchases were made between 2015 and 2022
summary: American Express agreed to pay $17.5 million to settle antitrust claims that it harmed consumers who used competing non-rewards credit cards at 38 major retailers in Illinois.

title: $5M Dapper Labs NFT Privacy Settlement
eligibility: Consumers who held an active Dapper Labs account (including NBA Top Shot, NFL All Day) between June 15, 2020 and January 30, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Had an active Dapper Labs account (e.g., NBA Top Shot, NFL All Day)
Account was active between June 15, 2020 and January 30, 2025
U.S. resident
summary: Dapper Labs agreed to pay $5 million to settle privacy claims related to its NFT platforms including NBA Top Shot. Each qualifying class member receives a flat $5 payment.
caseInfo: Dapper Labs NFT Privacy Class Action

title: $299.5M Toyota Forklift Emissions Settlement
eligibility: Individuals or businesses that purchased or leased a Toyota internal combustion (IC) forklift built between 2007 and 2021.
eligibilityBullets: Purchased or leased a Toyota IC forklift
Forklift was built between 2007 and 2021
Located in the United States
proofDetails: Proof of purchase or lease of qualifying Toyota IC forklift including serial number or purchase records.
summary: Toyota agreed to a $299.5 million settlement over claims that its IC forklifts did not meet emissions standards. Qualifying owners can receive $1,000-$2,500 per forklift plus a free service visit.

title: $5.25M Cadence Bank MOVEit Data Breach Settlement
eligibility: Individuals whom Cadence Bank notified about the MOVEit data breach that compromised their personal information.
eligibilityBullets: Received a data breach notification from Cadence Bank
Personal information was compromised in the MOVEit data breach
U.S. resident
proofDetails: Data breach notification letter from Cadence Bank. Documented losses for claims above base payment.
summary: Cadence Bank agreed to a $5.25 million settlement to resolve claims related to the MOVEit file transfer data breach. Eligible class members can claim up to $12,500.
caseInfo: Cadence Bank MOVEit Data Breach Litigation

title: $10M Farmers Insurance Agent Settlement
eligibility: Employees who worked as a Farmers Insurance agent outside California between March 9, 2020 and September 30, 2025.
eligibilityBullets: Worked as a Farmers Insurance agent
Employment was outside California
Worked between March 9, 2020 and September 30, 2025
summary: Farmers Insurance agreed to a $10 million settlement to resolve labor claims from agents outside California alleging improper classification and wage practices.
